'''Keegan Hirst''' (born 13 February 1988) is an English professional [[rugby league]] player who played most recently as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], a personal trainer and a podcaster.

He played for the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] in [[2008 National League Two]], and the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in [[2009 Championship 1]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Hirst also played for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in two separate spells and for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the Championship. He played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Halifax RLFC|Halifax]] in the second tier. After the abandonment of the [[2020 RFL Championship|2020 Championship season]], Hirst signed for a third spell with Batley but in October 2020 announced his retirement from the game.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=Keegan Hirst announces retirement |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/keegan-hirst-announces-retirement/ |website=Love Rugby League |accessdate=8 October 2020 |date=8 October 2020}}</ref>

In 2015, he became the first British professional rugby league player to [[coming out|come out]] as [[Gay men|gay]].<ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |date=2022-10-28 |title=Spotlight: Keegan Hirst |url=https://queerinsider.com/featured/spotlight-keegan-hirst/ |access-date=2023-08-15 |website=Queer Insider |language=en-US}}</ref>

==Background==
Hirst was born in [[Batley]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Flanagan |first=Aaron |date=2015-08-16 |title=Who is Keegan Hirst? |url=http://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/who-keegan-hirst-rugby-leagues-6260953 |access-date=2024-04-18 |website=The Mirror |language=en}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Hirst began his career as an academy player at [[Bradford Bulls]] before joining [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/news_behind/13625179.How_rugby_star_Keegan_Hirst_s_coming_out_statement_helps_to_challenge_gay_stereotypes|title=How rugby star Keegan Hirst's coming out statement helps to challenge gay stereotypes|newspaper=[[Telegraph & Argus]]|date=25 August 2015|accessdate=31 August 2015}}</ref> After a season with the Hunslet Hawks, Hirst moved to [[Dewsbury Rams]] for two seasons and then moved to [[Batley Bulldogs]] for the 2012 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/11/roughyeds/21313/roughyeds-2009|title=Roughyeds 2009|newspaper=[[Oldham Evening Chronicle]]|date=11 March 2009|accessdate=31 August 2015}}</ref> In 2013 he moved to [[Featherstone Rovers]] but returned to Batley, first on loan and then on a permanent move in 2014.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/featherstone-rovers/rovers-prop-hirst-is-back-at-batley-1-6962183|title=Rovers prop Hirst is back at Batley|newspaper=Pontefract and Castleford Express|date=21 November 2014|accessdate=31 August 2015}}</ref>

In August 2016, Hirst signed a deal with [[Wakefield Trinity]] to play for the [[Super League]] side from 2017.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37045442|title=Keegan Hirst: Batley Bulldogs prop agrees move to Wakefield Wildcats|work=BBC Sport|date=11 August 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Keegan Hirst extends Wakefield Trinity contract until 2019 |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/11277928/keegan-hirst-extends-wakefield-trinity-contract-until-2019 |access-date=2023-08-15 |website=Sky Sports |language=en}}</ref> Hirst made his Super League début in the round six match of [[Super League XXII]] against [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite news |url=http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity/wakefield-trinity-28-leigh-centurions-24-bish-clocks-up-a-ton-as-trinity-hit-back-to-win-1-8456682 |title=Wakefield Trinity 28 Leigh Centurions 24: Bish' clocks up a ton as Trinity hit back to win |newspaper=[[Yorkshire Evening Post]] |date=23 March 2017 |accessdate=24 March 2017}}</ref>

==In popular culture==
In 2016, Hirst was a dater on [[Channel 4]]'s dating show ''[[First Dates]]'',<ref>{{Cite web |last=McCormick |first=Joseph |date=2016-07-23 |title=Gay rugby star Keegan Hirst accused of having a boyfriend despite appearing on First Dates |url=https://www.thepinknews.com/2016/07/23/gay-rugby-star-keegan-hirst-accused-of-having-a-boyfriend-despite-appearing-on-first-dates/ |access-date=2023-08-15 |website=PinkNews |language=en-US}}</ref> also in 2016 Hirst took part in [[W (UK TV channel)|W]]'s ''Celebrity Haunted Hotel''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Celebrity guests check-in to W's Haunted Hotel |url=https://corporate.uktv.co.uk/news/article/celebrity-guests-check-ws-haunted-hotel/ |access-date=2023-08-15 |website=corporate.uktv.co.uk |language=en}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Hirst is divorced with two children.<ref name="Sunday Mirror">{{cite news|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-star-keegan-hirst-6260707|title=Rugby League star Keegan Hirst becomes first Brit player to come out as gay|newspaper=[[Sunday Mirror]]|date=15 August 2015|accessdate=31 August 2015}}</ref> In an interview with the ''[[Sunday Mirror]]'' in August 2015 he [[Coming out|came out]] as [[Gay men|gay]],<ref name="Sunday Mirror"/> becoming the first British professional [[rugby league]] player to do so.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/aug/16/keegan-hirst-first-rugby-league-player-come-out-gay|title=Keegan Hirst steps on to field as Britain's first rugby league star to come out as gay|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]|date=16 August 2015|accessdate=31 August 2015}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Maskell |first=Emily |date=2022-08-02 |title=First openly gay rugby league player Keegan Hirst comes out of retirement |url=https://www.attitude.co.uk/news/world/first-openly-gay-rugby-league-player-keegan-hirst-comes-out-of-retirement-403997/ |access-date=2023-08-15 |website=Attitude |language=en-GB}}</ref>

From May 2022, Hirst was in a relationship with British [[YouTuber]] Joel Wood<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.instagram.com/p/CidhliZImwP/ | title=Joel Wood on Instagram: "So you might've seen him on a few insta stories here and there but tonight I've just released our first vlog on my YouTube channel of our time in Edinburgh (Actually this is just Episode 1, there's more to come!) if you're a VIP member on my channel then you'll already have met @keeganhirst in a couple of videos but to everyone else… here ya go! Be nice to him please 😂" }}</ref>{{better source needed|reason=social media|date=March 2025}} <ref>{{cite web | website=[[YouTube]] | url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sWolqvNqXiw | title=Joel Wood with Hirst on YouTube: "Hey homos, welcome back to our podcast, Happy Healthy Homo. I'm Keegan and I'm Joel. We're boyfriends and this is Happy Healthy Homo, the podcast where we are empowering homos to live happily and healthily. " | date=18 September 2025 }}</ref> with whom he created in 2023 the podcast [[Happy Healthy Homo]],<ref>{{cite web | title=Happy Healthy Homo | url=https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/happy-healthy-homo/id1677092370 }}</ref> also available in a video version on YouTube,<ref>{{cite web | title=Happy Healthy Homo | website=[[YouTube]] | url=https://www.youtube.com/@happyhealthyhomo }}</ref> essentially aimed at LGBTQ+ listeners, but also who they inclusively call "allies" or more jokingly "honorary homos". In January 2026, Wood announced on Instagram that the couple had decided to end their relationship.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Wood |first=Joel |date=23 January 2026 |title=Instagram |url=https://www.instagram.com/p/DT3F9rEDJrV |access-date=2026-01-25 |website=www.instagram.com}}</ref>

In November 2024, Hirst was diagnosed with [[dilated cardiomyopathy]] and [[heart failure]] for which he needs a constant medication treatment.<ref>https://www.tiktok.com/@keegan_hirst/video/7462739399369919776</ref>
